'''House Vandron''' was considered to be the first among equals of the [[Ancient Houses]] that ruled the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. It was the oldest and most noble of the Houses, and named for the founder of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]], [[Thull Vandron]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Its homeworld was [[Karfeddion/Legends|Karfeddion]], and shared the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Asmeru/Legends|Asmeru]] with [[House Elegin/Legends|House Elegin]]. As of [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], it was ruled by [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Crueya Vandron/Legends|Crueya Vandron]] and his wife [[Theala Vandron|Lady Theala]]. Under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], Crueya Vandron became the founder of [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]]; but his daughter, also called [[Theala Vandron Jr.|Theala Vandron]], had succeeded to the title by [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].

House Vandron owned a sizable naval fleet, containing [[Tikiar (starfighter)/Legends|Tikiar fighters]] and [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaught cruisers]]. House Vandron sustained itself by selling its agricultural produce, breeding and selling the Human and [[Ossan]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] that worked said agriculture, and using its fleet for extortion and piracy.

A member of House Vandron was at one point about to engage in a marriage with [[Tia Organa]], but the proposed alliance was broken off following a scandal. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], they were key backers of [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren's]] attempt to set her son [[Irek Ismaren|Irek]] up as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].
